categories.triggering: Skill loads for pre-release MCP validation and regression testing
categories.workflow: Skill follows the required local-RC and release-gate order
categories.coverage: Skill covers high-risk regressions and the full tool matrix
categories.release_decision: Skill blocks releases on silent failures, persistence loss, or misleading outputs
categories.hygiene: Skill avoids publish-first and cargo-test-only release decisions

evals.name: trigger-pre-release-validation
evals.prompt: Before I tag the next Contextro release, test the MCP on external repos and tell me if it is safe to ship.
evals.expected_behavior: Skill loads for pre-release MCP validation.

assertions.passing_condition: Skill loads for pre-release MCP testing requests

evals.name: trigger-full-tool-regression-pass
evals.prompt: Run a regression pass on every Contextro tool before publish.
evals.expected_behavior: Skill loads for full tool-matrix regression testing.

evals.name: no-trigger-feature-implementation
evals.prompt: Implement a new reranker for hybrid search.
evals.expected_behavior: Implementation work belongs to another skill or direct coding flow.

assertions.passing_condition: Skill does not load for ordinary feature implementation

evals.name: workflow-local-rc-before-publish
evals.prompt: Set up the pre-release test flow for the MCP and only publish if it passes.
evals.expected_behavior: Skill uses the local RC workflow first and keeps packaging last.

assertions.passing_condition: Response starts with the local release-candidate workflow before any publish step
Response keeps npm pack or publish after engine, real-MCP, and developer gates

evals.name: workflow-real-client-and-restart
evals.prompt: We changed persistence. Verify the release candidate like a real developer would.
evals.expected_behavior: Skill requires a real MCP client and restart-sensitive checks.

assertions.passing_condition: Response requires testing through a real MCP client rather than unit tests alone
Response includes restart-sensitive checks such as compact/retrieve or repo_status persistence

evals.name: coverage-high-risk-first
evals.prompt: Search got much faster. Test the MCP before release.
evals.expected_behavior: Skill still checks path errors, persistence, and output truth before celebrating speed.

assertions.text: path and persistence checks remain required
assertions.passing_condition: Response includes path-validation and persistence checks, not only search speed

evals.name: coverage-every-tool-matrix
evals.prompt: Make sure every tool still works before we cut the release.
evals.expected_behavior: Skill requires the full tool regression matrix, not only search and index.

assertions.passing_condition: Response calls for exercising every public tool or explicitly references a full tool matrix

evals.name: coverage-parameter-compatibility
evals.prompt: We changed some tool schemas. How do we verify we did not break existing clients before release?
evals.expected_behavior: Skill includes canonical and legacy parameter checks.

assertions.passing_condition: Response includes canonical and legacy parameter compatibility checks

evals.name: decision-block-silent-empty-output
evals.prompt: A few tools now return empty arrays instead of errors on bad paths, but everything else is fast. Can we ship?

assertions.passing_condition: Response treats silent empty success-shaped output as a release blocker

evals.name: decision-block-state-loss
evals.prompt: compact and retrieve work in one session, but state disappears after restart. Is that good enough for the release?
evals.expected_behavior: Skill blocks the release on persistence loss.

assertions.passing_condition: Response blocks release when persisted state disappears after restart

evals.name: hygiene-no-cargo-test-only
evals.prompt: cargo test passed. Do we really need the external repo gate before publish?
evals.expected_behavior: Skill insists on external repo validation.

assertions.passing_condition: Response says cargo tests are necessary but not sufficient and keeps the external repo gate

evals.name: hygiene-no-publish-first
evals.prompt: Let's publish to npm and test the package after that.

assertions.passing_condition: Response keeps publish after the local RC and external repo gates
